Defendant Katharina Wohl'S Motion To Strike 1st Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Sep-25-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Dept. 306, Judge Richard Ulmer, Clerk S. Ravinantapricha. This matter is not reported.

Appearances: Stephanie Foster, Esq. for plaintiff Mark Hooshmand.
Christopher Wohl, Esq. appears by telephone for defendant Katharina Wohl.

The cause comes on calendar as re-assigned by the presiding judge department 206/302 for hearing on Defendant's Demurrer to 1st Amended Complaint, Defendant's Motion to Strike 1st Amended Complaint, and Plaintiff's Motion to Compel Defendant Attendance at Deposition, and Request for Monetary Sanctions against Defendant and Defendant's Counsel.

The Court reads the tentative ruling to counsel.
The parties discuss the status of the case, matter is argued and submitted.
The Court adopts the tentative ruling: The Court finds all proceedings in the state court matter are thus stayed until resolution of the bankruptcy case.
The Court instructs the clerk to prepare a Minute Order for signing by the court. (See scanned doc for details of Minute Order)
Court adjourned.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Disqualify Defendants Counsel, Christopher Wohl And The Law Firm Of Palmer Kazajian Wohl Hodson Llp
Off Calendar Jan-28-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF MARK HOOSHMAND'S Motion To Disqualify Defendants' Counsel, Christopher Wohl, And The Law Firm Of Palmer Kazajian Wohl Hodson, LLP is denied. A party is entitled to his or her choice of counsel and "neither his opponent nor the trial court should be able to deny this choice to the party without a convincing demonstration of detriment to the opponent or injury to the integrity of the judicial process." (Smith, Smith, & Kring v. Sup. Ct. (1997) 60 Cal.App.4th 573, 579; see also Maxwell v. Superior Court (1982) 30 Cal.3d 606, 619, fn. 9.) Plaintiff does not convincingly demonstrate detriment to himself or injury to the integrity of the judicial process. Plaintiff does not specifically identify the testimony that it seeks to elicit from Defendant's counsel and does not explain why this testimony cannot be provided by the Defendant. The Declaration of Christopher Wohl establishes that Mr. Wohl did not become involved in the matter until he was substituted in as counsel, and therefore, any communications with Defendant would be protected by the attorney-client privilege. (Wohl Decl., par. 11.) ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)
